hero.question: UFC Fight Night: Tresean Gore vs. Azamat Bekoev (Middleweight, Prelims)
hero.prob_take: The market has priced Gore as a near-lock at 87.5%, which means you're laying heavy juice on a preliminary fighter in a sport where upsets happen at 15% frequency. Bekoev is a live underdog at these odds if Gore has any injury concerns, cardio questions, or stylistic vulnerability that hasn't fully baked into the consensus yet.

daily_take.headline: Gore's Odds Explode 71 Points in 24 Hours. Smart Money Knows Something.
daily_take.deck: Tresean Gore just ripped from 16.5% to 87.5% on $105K volume, a move this violent doesn't happen on noise. Either new injury intel dropped on Bekoev, or sharp bettors saw something in recent footage that flipped the matchup. The question is whether this repricing exhausted the edge or if there's more to run. Watch the next 6 hours for volume patterns, not narrative. If big money keeps stacking Gore, you're chasing. If volume dies, you're looking at a real opportunity to fade the move.
